page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, this server features a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded decompression, and memory bandwidth across both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks. It also achieves top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations, including Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is more moderate, ranking in the average tier for stress-ng single-core tests and image processing, indicating that workloads must be highly parallelized to fully exploit the hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture and high physical core density offer a highly efficient platform for multi-threaded workloads without hyperthreading contention. The inclusion of 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local data access without the added cost of external block storage. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core, which makes this server less suitable for memory-intensive applications despite its high compute capacity.

This server is optimized for highly parallelized, compute-heavy workloads that benefit from direct hardware access and local high-speed storage. Suitable use cases include large-scale software compilation, multi-threaded database operations, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores of the 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory, a 5700 GB local NVMe SSD, and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Lacking hyperthreading, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance remains average. The low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB represents a tradeoff for memory-heavy tasks, but the high core density and bundled local storage make it highly cost-efficient for parallelized workloads. It is best suited for software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 Graviton ARMv9 cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server featuring 96 Graviton ARMv9 cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A bare-metal, ARM-based compute platform featuring ninety-six dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe storage for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated physical AWS Graviton ARMv9 cores with no hyperthreading overhead
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
5700 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
